name: FC BATE Borisov
content:
FC BATE Borisov is one of the most successful and well-known football clubs in Belarus, based in the city of Barysaw. Founded in 1973, the club currently competes in the Belarusian Premier League and plays its home matches at the Borisov Arena, opened in 2014. BATE is widely recognized for its distinctive yellow-and-blue colors and strong domestic presence.

Domestically, BATE Borisov has been the dominant force in Belarusian football, winning a record 15 league titles, including an extraordinary run of 13 consecutive championships between 2006 and 2018. In addition to their league success, the club has also won multiple Belarusian Cups and Super Cups, cementing its status as the country’s most decorated team.

On the international stage, BATE has achieved notable success for a Belarusian club. It is the only team from Belarus to have reached the group stage of the UEFA Champions League on multiple occasions and has also appeared several times in the UEFA Europa League group stage. These achievements have made BATE Borisov a regular representative of Belarus in European competitions and a key contributor to the country’s football reputation abroad.

name: FC Dinamo Minsk
content:
FC Dinamo Minsk is one of the oldest and most historic football clubs in Belarus, based in Minsk and founded in 1927 as part of the Dinamo sports society. It currently competes in the Belarusian Premier League and is traditionally associated with white and blue colors. The club was the only representative of the Belarusian SSR to play extensively in the Soviet Top League, where it spent many seasons competing at the highest level.

During the Soviet era, Dinamo Minsk achieved its greatest success by winning the Soviet Top League title in 1982, marking a historic milestone for Belarusian football. The club also enjoyed notable European runs in the 1980s, reaching the quarter-finals of major UEFA competitions such as the European Cup and UEFA Cup. These achievements established Dinamo Minsk as one of the most respected teams from the Soviet Union at the time.

After Belarus gained independence in 1991, Dinamo Minsk became the dominant force in the newly formed domestic league, winning the inaugural Belarusian Premier League in 1992 and securing multiple titles throughout the 1990s. Although later overshadowed by clubs like BATE Borisov, Dinamo Minsk remains one of the most decorated teams in Belarus, with numerous league titles and domestic trophies, and continues to be a regular participant in European competitions.

name: FC Shakhtyor Soligorsk
content:
FC Shakhtyor Soligorsk is a Belarusian football club based in the mining city of Soligorsk, founded in 1961. The club has been a consistent participant in the Belarusian Premier League since 1992, following the country’s independence. During the Soviet era, Shakhtyor competed in regional competitions and achieved notable success, including multiple Belarusian SSR Cup victories in the 1980s.

After struggling in the 1990s, the club established itself as one of the top teams in Belarus from the late 1990s onward. Shakhtyor became regular participants in European competitions and achieved major domestic success, winning the Belarusian Premier League titles in 2005, 2020, and 2021. They also finished as runners-up several times and won the Belarusian Cup on multiple occasions, confirming their status as one of the country’s strongest clubs.

However, the club faced serious difficulties in recent years. In 2023, Shakhtyor was involved in a match-fixing scandal that led to the stripping of their 2022 title and significant point deductions. After finishing last in the 2024 season and being relegated for the first time, the situation worsened, and in 2025 the club was ultimately disbanded due to financial issues and failure to obtain a license.

name: FC Slavia Mozyr
content:
FC Slavia Mozyr is a Belarusian football club based in the city of Mozyr, founded in 1987 under the name Polesie Mozyr. The team currently competes in the Belarusian Premier League and plays its home matches at Yunost Stadium. After the dissolution of the Soviet Union, the club joined the Belarusian league system and gradually rose through the ranks, earning promotion to the top division in 1995.

The club enjoyed its golden era in the late 1990s and early 2000s. In their debut top-flight season, they finished as runners-up and also won the Belarusian Cup. Slavia Mozyr went on to win the Belarusian Premier League twice, in 1996 and 2000, becoming one of the first clubs to break the early dominance of Dinamo Minsk. They also secured domestic cup victories during this period and participated in European competitions, including UEFA qualifying rounds.

After their peak years, the club experienced decline, including relegation in 2005 and financial difficulties that led to a merger with another team before eventually restoring its original identity. In recent years, Slavia Mozyr has moved between divisions but remains a stable participant in Belarusian football, regularly competing in the top tier and maintaining its reputation as a historic club with notable past achievements.

name: FC Gomel
content:
FC Gomel is a Belarusian football club based in the city of Gomel, founded in 1959 under the name Lokomotiv Gomel. The team currently competes in the Belarusian Premier League and plays its home matches at the Central Stadium. Throughout its history, the club has undergone several name changes before adopting its current identity in 1995.

During the Soviet era, the club competed mainly in the lower divisions, including the second and third tiers of Soviet football. After Belarus gained independence in 1991, Gomel became a founding member of the Belarusian Premier League in 1992, although its early years were inconsistent, including relegation in 1995. The team returned to the top flight in 1998 and began to establish itself as a competitive side.

The club’s most successful period came in the early 2000s. FC Gomel won its first and only Belarusian Premier League title in 2003 and also secured the Belarusian Cup in 2002, later reaching the final again in 2004. They have also participated in European competitions, including a notable UEFA Europa League qualifying tie against Liverpool in 2012. Today, Gomel remains a solid mid-to-upper table team in Belarusian football with a respected history.

name: FC Belshina Bobruisk
content:
FC Belshina Bobruisk is a Belarusian football club based in the city of Bobruisk, founded in 1976 (originally as Shinnik Bobruisk). The team plays its home matches at Spartak Stadium and has long been associated with the local tire manufacturer Belshina, which also inspired the club’s current name adopted in 1996. Over the years, the club has competed in both the Belarusian Premier League and the First League, moving between divisions depending on performance.

The club enjoyed its most successful period in the late 1990s and early 2000s. Belshina won the Belarusian Premier League title in 2001, having previously finished as runners-up in 1997. During the same era, they also achieved strong cup success, winning the Belarusian Cup three times (1997, 1999, 2001), which established them as one of the notable teams in Belarusian football at the time.

In more recent years, the club has been less consistent, often competing outside the top tier but continuing to remain part of the national football structure. Despite fluctuations, Belshina has periodically returned to the Premier League, including promotion via play-offs for the 2026 season. Today, the club is regarded as a historic Belarusian team with a notable peak era and a continued presence in domestic competitions.

name: FC Dnepr Mogilev
content:
FC Dnepr Mogilev is a Belarusian football club based in Mogilev, founded in 1960. The team plays its home matches at Spartak Stadium and currently competes in the Belarusian Premier League after earning promotion in 2025. Over its history, the club has undergone several name changes, including Khimik Mogilev and Spartak Mogilev, before adopting its current name in 1973.

During the Soviet era, Dnepr mostly played in the lower tiers of the league system, with a brief promotion to the second tier in 1982. After Belarus gained independence, the club became a founding member of the Belarusian Premier League in 1992 and quickly achieved success, finishing as runners-up that same year and winning the league title in 1998—its greatest achievement to date. The club has also had appearances in European competitions, including the UEFA Champions League qualifiers and UEFA Europa League.

In recent years, the club has experienced instability, including relegations and organizational changes. In 2019, it merged with Luch Minsk to form Dnyapro Mogilev, but the project lasted only one season before dissolution. The original club was re-established in 2020 and worked its way back up the league system, eventually returning to the top division. Today, Dnepr Mogilev is considered a historic Belarusian club with a notable past and a renewed presence in domestic football.

name: FC Dynamo Brest
content:
FC Dynamo Brest is a Belarusian football club based in the city of Brest, founded in 1960. The team currently competes in the Belarusian Premier League and plays its home matches at the Regional Sport Complex Brestsky. Over its history, the club has undergone several name changes, including Spartak Brest, Bug Brest, and later Dynamo Brest, reflecting both regional identity and its affiliation with the Dinamo sports society.

During the Soviet era, Dynamo Brest mainly competed in the lower tiers of the league system, with occasional promotions but no sustained top-level presence. After Belarus gained independence in 1991, the club became a regular participant in the Belarusian Premier League from its inaugural season in 1992 and has remained there ever since. For many years, the team was considered competitive but not dominant, often finishing behind stronger clubs like BATE Borisov.

The club’s greatest achievement came in 2019, when Dynamo Brest won its first Belarusian Premier League title, ending BATE Borisov’s long dominance. In addition to this historic triumph, the club has also enjoyed success in domestic cup competitions, winning the Belarusian Cup multiple times (including 2007, 2017, and 2018). Dynamo Brest has also participated in European competitions such as the UEFA Champions League and UEFA Europa League qualifiers, helping raise its profile internationally.

name: FC Maxline Vitebsk
content:
FC Maxline Vitebsk is a Belarusian football club based in Vitebsk. The club has a relatively complex history, originally founded in 1983 in Rogachev under different names and undergoing several rebrandings over the decades. It was re-established in modern form in 2014 and later came under the sponsorship of the betting company Maxline, which gave the team its recent identity. In 2023, the club relocated from Rogachev to Vitebsk, aiming to benefit from better infrastructure and a larger football market.

In recent years, Maxline Vitebsk has risen rapidly through the Belarusian football system. After competing in the First League, the club achieved promotion to the Belarusian Premier League for the first time following strong performances in the 2024 season. The team continued its momentum and even won its first top-flight title in 2025, marking a remarkable breakthrough for such a newly established Premier League side.

The club is known for its heavy investment compared to many Belarusian teams, allowing it to quickly build a competitive squad. However, its rapid rise has also sparked discussion in Belarusian football due to its corporate ownership model and fast relocation history. Despite that, Maxline Vitebsk has already established itself as one of the most surprising new forces in Belarusian football.

name: FC Neman Grodno
content:
FC Neman Grodno is a Belarusian football club based in the city of Grodno, founded in 1964. The team currently competes in the Belarusian Premier League and plays its home matches at Neman Stadium. The club is named after the Neman River and has spent most of its modern history in the top tier of Belarusian football since independence in 1992.

Historically, Neman Grodno developed steadily but without major dominance in Soviet football, mainly playing in lower divisions. After joining the Belarusian league system, the club established itself as a consistent Premier League side. One of its early highlights was winning the Belarusian Cup in 1993, which remains an important achievement in its history. The club has also experienced strong periods, particularly in the early 2000s, when it challenged near the top of the league and finished close to title contention.

In recent years, Neman Grodno has become one of the more stable and competitive clubs in Belarus, regularly finishing in the upper part of the table and competing in European qualifiers. The team has also had several strong cup runs and continues to build a reputation as a solid top-flight side capable of challenging traditional Belarusian powerhouses like BATE Borisov and Dinamo Minsk.

name: FC Vitebsk
content:
FC Vitebsk is a Belarusian football club based in the city of Vitebsk, founded in 1960. The team currently competes in the Belarusian Premier League and plays its home matches at the Vitebsky Central Sport Complex. Throughout its history, the club has gone through numerous name changes and reorganizations, reflecting different eras of Soviet and post-Soviet football development.

During the Soviet period, the club mainly played in lower divisions, without reaching the top tier of Soviet football. After Belarus became independent, FC Vitebsk became one of the founding members of the Belarusian Premier League in 1992. In the early years of the league, the club was quite competitive, finishing as runners-up twice (1992–93 and 1994–95) and also placing third on several occasions. One of its key achievements is winning the Belarusian Cup in 1998.

In the following decades, FC Vitebsk experienced more instability, including relegations and promotions between divisions. While no longer among the dominant clubs in Belarus like BATE Borisov or Dinamo Minsk, it remains a well-established top-flight team with occasional European appearances. In recent seasons, the club has continued to compete in the Premier League, aiming to maintain stability and improve its position in Belarusian football.

name: FC Dinamo-93 Minsk
content:
FC Dinamo-93 Minsk was a Belarusian football club based in Minsk that existed from 1992 to 1998. The club originally started as a reserve team of Dinamo Minsk under the name Dinamo-2 Minsk. After winning the Belarusian First League in 1992, it separated from its parent club, became independent, and was first renamed Belarus Minsk before finally adopting the name Dinamo-93 Minsk in 1993.

Despite its short existence, Dinamo-93 Minsk was quite competitive in the early years of Belarusian football. The team finished as runners-up in the Belarusian Premier League once (1993–94) and also recorded several third-place finishes in the mid-1990s. They won the Belarusian Cup in 1995 and reached another final in 1997. Thanks to these results, the club also took part in European competitions such as the UEFA Cup Winners’ Cup and UEFA Cup, although without major success.

The club’s decline came quickly due to financial problems. In 1998, during the league season, Dinamo-93 Minsk withdrew from competition and was dissolved shortly after. Despite its short lifespan, the club is remembered as one of the early competitive sides in post-Soviet Belarusian football, briefly challenging the dominance of stronger Minsk-based teams.

name: FC Torpedo-BelAZ Zhodino
content:
FC Torpedo-BelAZ Zhodino is a Belarusian football club based in the city of Zhodino, founded in 1961. The team currently competes in the Belarusian Premier League and plays its home matches at Torpedo Stadium. The club is sponsored by the Belarusian industrial company BelAZ, which is reflected in its name and long-standing financial support.

Historically, Torpedo-BelAZ has been a stable top-flight team in Belarusian football, though it is not among the most dominant clubs like BATE Borisov or Dinamo Minsk. During the Soviet era, the club achieved success in the Belarusian SSR competitions, winning several regional titles in the 1970s and 1980s. After Belarus gained independence, it spent time in lower divisions before returning to the Premier League in the early 2000s, where it has mostly remained since.

In modern times, the club is considered a competitive mid-table side with occasional strong seasons. Its biggest achievements include winning the Belarusian Cup twice (2015–16 and 2022–23) and the Belarusian Super Cup in 2024. Torpedo-BelAZ has also participated in UEFA Europa League and Conference League qualifying rounds, gaining experience on the European stage despite limited success there.

name: FC Partizan Minsk
content:
FC Partizan Minsk was a Belarusian football club based in Minsk that existed from 2002 to 2014. The club was originally founded as MTZ-RIPO Minsk after the merger of two Minsk-based teams and was backed by the Minsk Tractor Works factory. It began in the lower divisions but quickly rose through the system, reaching the Belarusian Premier League in 2004.

During its peak years, Partizan Minsk became known as a competitive and unpredictable side in Belarusian football. The club won the Belarusian Cup twice (2005 and 2008), with notable victories over stronger teams like BATE Borisov and Shakhtyor Soligorsk in cup finals. It also achieved respectable league finishes in the mid-2000s and participated in European competitions, including UEFA Cup/Europa League qualifying rounds.

However, the club struggled financially after its main investor withdrew support around 2012. This led to the loss of key players, withdrawal from the top division, and eventual dissolution. After a brief attempt to continue in lower leagues, FC Partizan Minsk ceased operations completely in 2014.

name: FC Minsk
content:
FC Minsk is a Belarusian football club based in the capital city, Minsk, founded in 2006. The club was created after the merger and restructuring of local teams, including Smena Minsk and Torpedo-SKA Minsk, and it was immediately granted a place in the Belarusian First League before quickly reaching the top division.

In its early years, FC Minsk experienced rapid movement between divisions. After promotion to the Belarusian Premier League, the club was relegated in its debut top-flight season but returned soon after and established itself as a stable Premier League side. One of its best domestic performances came in 2010, when the club finished 3rd in the league, which also earned it qualification for European competitions for the first time.

In European football, FC Minsk has competed in UEFA Europa League qualifying rounds, achieving some notable results such as eliminating Valletta and St Johnstone in 2013 before being knocked out in the play-off round. Domestically, the club also won the Belarusian Cup in 2013, which remains its most significant trophy. Today, FC Minsk is considered a mid-table Belarusian Premier League club that regularly develops young players and competes for European qualification spots.

name: FC Naftan Novopolotsk
content:
FC Naftan Novopolotsk is a Belarusian football club based in the city of Novopolotsk, founded in 1963. The team plays its home matches at Atlant Stadium and is closely connected to the local oil industry, particularly the Naftan refinery, which has historically supported the club. Over the years, the club has undergone several name changes before settling on its current identity.

Naftan has spent most of its history moving between divisions in Belarusian football. After Belarus became independent, the club established itself in the domestic league system and has had multiple spells in the Belarusian Premier League. One of its strongest periods came in the 2000s and early 2010s, when the team became a competitive top-flight side and achieved notable cup success.

The club’s biggest achievements are winning the Belarusian Cup twice, in 2009 and 2012, which also earned them participation in European competitions such as the UEFA Europa League qualifiers. In recent years, Naftan has struggled with consistency and has alternated between the Premier League and First League, but it remains a well-established club in Belarusian football with a long history and strong regional identity.

name: FC Isloch Minsk Raion
content:
FC Isloch Minsk Raion is a Belarusian football club based in Minsk, founded in 2007. Although it officially represents Minsk Raion (Minsk District), the club plays its home matches in the capital and is named after the nearby Islach River. It started as an amateur team and competed in regional competitions before entering the national league system.

After joining the Belarusian Second League in 2012, Isloch quickly progressed through the system, earning promotion to the First League and later reaching the Belarusian Premier League for the first time in 2015. Since then, the club has established itself as a stable top-flight team, often finishing in mid-table positions but occasionally challenging for higher spots.

In cup competitions, Isloch has achieved some notable results, including reaching the semi-finals of the Belarusian Cup in 2019 after defeating BATE Borisov. The club has also participated in UEFA competitions such as the Europa Conference League qualifiers, marking its gradual growth on the domestic and European stage. However, its history also includes controversies, such as involvement in a match-fixing scandal in 2016 that led to sanctions for several individuals connected to the club.

name: FC Rechitsa-2014
content:
FC Rechitsa-2014 was a Belarusian football club based in the city of Rechytsa, in the Gomel Region. The club’s history dates back to 1952, when it was founded as Krasnaya Zvezda Rechitsa. Over the decades it went through several name changes, including Dneprovets, Dnepr, Neftyanik, Sputnik, Vedrich, and Vedrich-97, before finally becoming Rechitsa-2014 in 2014. The team played its home matches at the Central Stadium in Rechytsa.

After Belarus gained independence, the club was one of the early participants in the Belarusian football system and even became a founding member of the Belarusian Premier League in 1992. It spent several seasons in the top division during the 1990s and early 2000s, but often struggled near the bottom of the table and faced multiple relegations. Despite that, the club also had competitive moments in the First League and occasional strong cup runs.

In its final years, Rechitsa-2014 could not maintain stability. After finishing last in the First League in 2015 and again struggling in 2016, the club was relegated and subsequently dissolved in early 2016 due to financial and organizational difficulties. A new team later emerged in the city under the name Sputnik Rechitsa, continuing football traditions in the region in a different form.

name: FC Bobruisk
content:
FC Bobruisk was a Belarusian football club based in the city of Bobruisk that existed in the early 1990s. The club was originally founded in 1984 as Traktor Bobruisk, later renamed Fandok Bobruisk in 1992, and finally adopted the name FC Bobruisk in 1995. It played its home matches at Spartak Stadium in Bobruisk.

After the dissolution of the Soviet Union, FC Bobruisk competed in the Belarusian football system and became a founding participant in the Belarusian Premier League in 1992. The club had a brief period in the top division during the early years of Belarusian independence. Its best achievement came in the 1993–94 season, when it finished as runner-up in the Belarusian Cup, marking its only major domestic success.

However, the club struggled competitively and financially. In the 1995 season, FC Bobruisk finished last in the league with only two points, withdrew from competition, and was subsequently dissolved the same year. Today, football in Bobruisk is represented mainly by successor and other local clubs, most notably FC Belshina Bobruisk, which became the city’s main team.

name: FC Torpedo Mogilev
content:
FC Torpedo Mogilev is a Belarusian football club based in Mogilev. It was originally founded in 1959 under the name Kirovets Mogilev and later renamed Torpedo in 1974. The club played its home matches at Torpedo Stadium and has been closely linked with the city’s industrial background. Over time, it became one of the established teams in Mogilev football alongside other local clubs.

During the Soviet era, Torpedo Mogilev achieved one of its biggest successes by winning the Belarusian SSR League in 1982. After Belarus gained independence, the club became a founding member of the Belarusian Premier League in 1992. In the early seasons, Torpedo showed decent results, including mid-table finishes, but gradually declined in competitiveness through the 1990s, eventually struggling near the bottom of the table.

In the 2000s, the club was renamed Torpedo-Kadino Mogilev but continued to face financial and sporting difficulties. It was relegated to the First League and eventually disbanded in 2005. The club was later re-established in 2014 and briefly competed in lower divisions, but again faced financial issues and withdrew after the 2016 season. Today, it is mainly represented at youth and local level rather than professional competitions.

name: FC Torpedo Minsk
content:
FC Torpedo Minsk is a Belarusian football club based in the capital city, Minsk. The club was founded in 1947 and has a long, complex history with several re-establishments and name changes, including periods as Torpedo-MAZ and Torpedo-SKA. It plays its home matches at Torpedo Stadium, a traditional venue in Minsk that has been closely associated with the club since its early years.

During the Soviet era, Torpedo Minsk was a strong regional side, winning several Belarusian SSR championships (notably in the late 1940s and 1960s). After Belarus became independent, the club became a founding member of the Belarusian Premier League in 1992. In the early 2000s, it experienced one of its most competitive periods, finishing 4th twice and reaching the Belarusian Cup final in 2000.

However, the club has also faced repeated financial instability. In 2005 it was dissolved due to lack of funding, later reformed, and returned through the lower divisions. It reached the Premier League again in 2018 but withdrew mid-season due to financial problems. Since then, Torpedo Minsk has mostly competed in lower leagues, maintaining its identity as a historic but unstable Belarusian club.

name: FC SKVICh Minsk
content:
FC SKVICh Minsk was a Belarusian football club based in Minsk that existed from 2000 to 2014. The club was originally founded as Lokomotiv Minsk after being created with support from the SKVICh travel company and later backed by Belarusian Railways, which influenced its early identity and name. It played its home matches at Lokomotiv Stadium in Minsk.

In sporting terms, SKVICh was known as a classic “yo-yo club,” frequently moving between divisions. It quickly rose through the system, earning promotion to the Belarusian Premier League after winning the Second League in 2001 and finishing high in the First League in 2002. However, the team struggled to stay in the top division, being relegated multiple times throughout the 2000s while occasionally bouncing back after strong First League seasons.

One of the club’s best achievements came in cup competition, where it reached the Belarusian Cup final in 2003, losing to Dinamo Minsk. Financial instability and inconsistent results eventually led to decline, and after finishing poorly in the lower divisions, the club was officially dissolved in 2014.
